message:
feat(parser,core,vdbe): schema-qualified TableStar end-to-end + collation-aware REPLACE index delete path

Close out the schema-qualified TableStar work with a refactor of
the parser branch that's easier to audit, an end-to-end test
against an ATTACHed schema, and a collation-aware index exact-
match helper for the REPLACE path that was still doing bytewise
comparisons.

crates/fsqlite-parser/src/parser.rs

- parse_result_column() TableStar branch rewritten.  The previous
  form collapsed the two shapes (table.* and schema.table.*)
  into a single lookahead condition plus a `parse_qualified_name`
  call that silently walked both tokens.  That made the
  "did we match bare table.* or schema.table.*" decision
  implicit and hard to audit.
- The new form makes the two shapes explicit:
    if peek_nth(2) == Star {
        let table = parse_identifier()?;
        expect(Dot)?; expect(Star)?;
        return TableStar(QualifiedName::bare(table));
    }
    if peek_nth(2) is Id/QuotedId AND peek_nth(3) == Dot AND peek_nth(4) == Star {
        let schema = parse_identifier()?;
        expect(Dot)?;
        let table = parse_identifier()?;
        expect(Dot)?; expect(Star)?;
        return TableStar(QualifiedName::qualified(schema, table));
    }
- Using parse_identifier() + explicit expect(Dot)/expect(Star)
  at every step means any lexer drift (e.g. a stray whitespace
  token that shouldn't exist) surfaces as a structured parser
  error instead of the previous "identifier consumed more
  tokens than I expected" silent drift.

crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs

- New end-to-end regression test
  test_select_table_star_from_attached_schema_reads_all_columns:
    1. Open an in-memory connection.
    2. ATTACH a second in-memory database as `aux`.
    3. CREATE TABLE aux.t(id, value).
    4. INSERT two rows.
    5. Run `SELECT aux.t.* FROM aux.t ORDER BY id;` and assert
       both the column count and the row values match.
  Before this commit the parser would accept `aux.t.*` but the
  query planner would fall back to a bare `t` match and fail
  to find the ATTACHed table.  The test pins the full happy
  path end-to-end.

crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs

=== REPLACE secondary-index delete: new collation-aware probe ===

- New helper storage_cursor_find_exact_index_key(cursor_id,
  key_bytes, missing_registry_detail) -> Result<bool>:
    - Looks up the index root page + desc flags + collations
      via the usual cursor_root_pages lookup.
    - Decides uses_collated_probe from the collations vec
      (same rule as the existing NoConflict / IdxInsert paths).
    - If collated: snapshot the registry, delegate to the new
      storage_cursor_exact_match_collated helper below, return
      Err(Internal { detail }) on registry-miss so the caller
      sees a structured error rather than a panic.
    - If not collated: call the existing
      sc.cursor.index_move_to(&cx, key_bytes).is_found() path.
  The missing_registry_detail argument lets each caller supply
  an identifying error message.

- native_replace_row / delete_secondary_index_entries:
    Previously the REPLACE path deleted a row from the primary
    table cursor but used a bytewise sc.cursor.index_move_to
    call to find and delete the matching row in each secondary
    index.  Under NOCASE/RTRIM collations, "Foo" and "foo" are
    the same row from the table's perspective but different
    bytewise keys in the index, so the old bytewise probe
    would silently skip the secondary index entry and leave a
    dangling reference.
    Now route through storage_cursor_find_exact_index_key and
    the collation-aware exact match helper, with an appropriate
    detail string
    ("delete_secondary_index_entries: missing collation
    registry for collated exact probe") for the error path.
- The two additional REPLACE-related call sites
  (~line 8656 and 8707) get the same treatment.
- ~line 9582 (the UPDATE-path secondary index key search) also
  gets the new helper so UPDATE under NOCASE UNIQUE constraints
  finds the right row.

- New top-level helper storage_cursor_exact_match_collated
  (~line 13548) mirrors the prefix-match helper added in the
  earlier NoConflict commit but looks for an exact multi-column
  match under the given collations.  Returns Ok(true) on the
  first Equal match, Ok(false) after walking the full cursor.

- New tests exercise the collation-aware REPLACE path:
    REPLACE on a table with a NOCASE UNIQUE index correctly
    finds and removes the conflicting row so the REPLACE
    insert succeeds.
    DELETE ... WHERE name = 'foo' against a NOCASE UNIQUE
    column finds the 'Foo' row via the collated helper.
    ON CONFLICT REPLACE with a composite NOCASE + RTRIM index
    correctly canonicalizes both fields before the probe.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
